title: Cleisostoma linearilobatum
taxonomy: Cleisostoma linearilobatum is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Cleisostoma, which is a group of orchids that are native to tropical Asia. Cleisostoma linearilobatum is further classified as a member of the subtribe Cleisotinae. Within the subtribe, it is part of the tribe Cranichideae. This classification places Cleisostoma linearilobatum within the larger context of orchid systematics.

intro: Cleisostoma linearilobatum, also known as the Linear-lobed Cleisostoma, is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Cleisostoma linearilobatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cleisostoma linearilobatum is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture, often in combination with other herbs, to treat various health conditions.
